fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Iowa Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity iowa, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to iowa, respiratory therapist compact iowa, iowa respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Iowa Board of Respiratory Care and Polysomnography
board.parent_dept: Department of Inspections, Appeals, and Licensing (DIAL)

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) exam

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Iowa.

steps.title: Complete an Accredited Respiratory Care Program
steps.description: Graduate from a respiratory care education program accredited by the Commission on Accreditation for Respiratory Care (CoARC).

steps.title: Pass the NBRC Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) examination.

steps.title: Apply for an Iowa Respiratory Care License
steps.description: Submit an application for licensure to the Iowa Board of Respiratory Care and Polysomnography along with the required fee and documentation.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Iowa respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Iowa have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Iowa. To work in Iowa, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Iowa?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Iowa
hero.dek: Iowa Respiratory Therapist license requires graduation from an accredited program, passing the NBRC exam, and completing an application.
